idea.hook: Want to visit our nearest star, Proxima Centauri?
facts: It's 4.24 light-years away. That's over 40 TRILLION kilometers!
The fastest spacecraft EVER, NASA's Parker Solar Probe (690,000 km/h), would take over 6,600 years to get there.
Even traveling at the ultimate speed limit – the speed of light – it would still take 4.24 years.
idea.payoff: Interstellar tourism? Not even for your great-great-great-great-grandkids. We're still light-years away from true warp drive.
idea.title: Star Travel Reality Check! 🤯🚀

scenes.text:
Want to visit our nearest star?
Proxima Centauri?
scenes.visual: Starts with Earth visible, then zooms out rapidly to show a single, bright star (Proxima Centauri) in the distance against a dark starry background.

scenes.text:
It's 4.24 light-years away.
That's over 40 TRILLION kilometers!
scenes.visual: Two distinct star icons (Sun and Proxima Centauri) with a dashed line expanding between them. Numbers '4.24 light-years' then '40 TRILLION km' appear along the line.

scenes.text:
Fastest ever: NASA's Parker Solar Probe (690,000 km/h)
Would take over 6,600 YEARS!
scenes.visual: A small, futuristic spacecraft icon shoots from left to right across the screen, leaving a faint trail. A digital clock graphic appears, rapidly counting up to '6,600 YEARS'.

scenes.text:
Even at the ultimate speed limit:
SPEED OF LIGHT
It would still take 4.24 YEARS!
scenes.visual: A vibrant, pulsating beam of light streaks across the screen from left to right. The words 'SPEED OF LIGHT' flash brightly, followed by '4.24 YEARS'.

scenes.text:
Interstellar tourism?
Not for your great-great-great-great-grandkids.
Warp drive: still light-years away!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, slowly swirling nebula or galaxy cluster, emphasizing the vastness and mystery of space. Text appears sequentially with a subtle shimmer.
